settings.heading: Medal Sizes Guide: Standard Diameters in India
settings.subheading: What size should your medals be? A guide to standard medal diameters (50mm, 60mm, 70mm and finisher sizes), how size affects cost, and which to pick for your event. By Meru Trophy.

settings.label: Quick answer
settings.text: Standard medal sizes in India are around 50mm, 60mm and 70mm diameter, with larger finisher medals running 70–90mm+. Bigger medals feel more premium and cost a little more. Meru Trophy makes all sizes from ₹25. WhatsApp +91 78922 07252.

settings.heading: Common medal sizes
settings.text:
• 50mm: compact and economical; ideal for high-volume school sports days.
  • 60mm: the popular all-round size for tournaments and events.
  • 70mm: a more substantial, premium feel.
  • Finisher medals (70–90mm+): large keepsake pieces for marathons and flagship events.

settings.heading: Size vs cost
settings.text: Larger medals use more material and feel more premium, so they cost a little more per piece. For big groups on a budget, 50–60mm is the sweet spot; for keepsake finisher medals, go 70mm+. Ribbon length scales with medal size too.

settings.question: What's the standard medal size?
settings.answer: Around 50–70mm diameter; 60mm is the popular all-round choice. Finisher medals run larger (70–90mm+).

settings.question: Does a bigger medal cost more?
settings.answer: Yes — slightly, as it uses more material. We'll quote by your chosen size and quantity.

settings.question: Which size for a school sports day?
settings.answer: 50–60mm balances feel and budget for high volumes.

settings.question: Which size for marathon finishers?
settings.answer: 70mm and up, so the medal feels like a keepsake.

settings.heading: Need medals sized for your event?
settings.text: Tell us what you're planning — we'll share designs and a quote, usually within the hour.
